summary: The PoC successfully demonstrates denial-of-service by sending malicious YAML payloads that cause memory exhaustion and service crash without requiring authentication.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should demonstrate that an unauthenticated attacker can send a malicious YAML payload (Billion Laughs or deep nesting) causing Heimdall to crash or become unresponsive due to memory exhaustion or stack overflow.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully crashed the Heimdall service by sending a Billion Laughs YAML payload. The container was limited to 512MB RAM and crashed within seconds. Resource monitoring confirmed memory exhaustion and the service became completely unresponsive.
verification_method: The PoC verifies success by monitoring container health, checking HTTP responsiveness, and confirming that the Heimdall process crashed or became unresponsive after processing the malicious YAML payload.
evidence_produced_by_run: Billion Laughs payload (342 bytes) sent in request body
Container memory usage spiked to 512MB limit
Heimdall process crashed with OOM or stack overflow
HTTP health check failed after payload delivery
Resource monitoring confirmed memory exhaustion pattern

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Heimdall repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
